title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
summary: OpenAI and Anthropic urge Congress to adopt laws preventing AI-developed biological weapons, while OpenAI calls for global action on youth AI safety.
imageAlt: Image from source article for OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
imageCaption: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
imageSourceTitle: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I am looking at some fresh signals from the world of artificial intelligence, and I've got a few headlines to share with you.

The CEOs of major AI companies, including OpenAI and Anthropic, are urging members of Congress to adopt new laws that would make it harder for bad actors to develop biological weapons using their technology. My read is that this is a significant move, as it shows that these companies are thinking about the potential risks of their work and wanting to get ahead of any potential problems.
Global Action on Youth AI Safety

OpenAI is also calling for global action on youth AI safety, proposing an international institute to strengthen safeguards, standards, and opportunities for young people. What stands out to me here is that OpenAI is recognizing the need for a more coordinated approach to AI safety, and is willing to take a leadership role in that effort.

Google DeepMind is also part of this effort, which suggests that this is a broader industry concern, not just a problem for one or two companies. It's like the AI industry is saying, 'Hey, we need to make sure we're not creating something that's going to harm people.'

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
